fitScoreNote: Dan is an AI builder and GTM leader who has already shipped what this role is responsible for designing, a fully deployed agent platform for a commercial GTM team with usage tracking and adoption measurement. You can see a prototype of the platform at the Notion Meeting Intelligence system link below. Based in SF, so working in person will not be an issue. He's an avid user of Notion both for work and in his personal life, and would love to offer his expertise in many areas to help his favorite software and developer platform achieve their goals.
jobDescriptionText:
Role: GTM AI + Innovation Manager
Department: Revenue Operations & Strategy

About Notion
Notion helps you build beautiful tools for your life's work. Notion provides one place for teams to get everything done, seamlessly connecting docs, notes, projects, calendar, and email -- with AI built in to find answers and automate work. Millions of users, from individuals to large organizations like Toyota, Figma, and OpenAI, love Notion for its flexibility.

About the Role
As GTM AI & Innovation Manager, you will support revenue growth by architecting and implementing AI-powered solutions that enhance our GTM team's effectiveness and accelerate customer acquisition. Working closely with GTM teams, Business Technology, Data Science and Automations Engineering, you'll help develop innovative approaches to leverage AI for improving sales processes and optimizing customer outcomes.

What You'll Achieve
- Systems Thinking: Understand how to design systems that meet field sellers, marketers and post-sales resources where they do their daily work
- Hands-On Building: Work alongside engineering to build out prototype and production-grade systems, ship continuously
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Bridge technical and commercial teams, gathering technical requirements while ensuring solutions deliver real business value
- Ongoing Enablement: Communicate enhancements to revenue teams and encourage adoption through continuous learning
- ROI Measurement: Track and optimize the pipeline and revenue impact of AI and automation implementations across GTM

Skills You'll Need
- 3-5 years of work experience in a technical commercial role (sales engineer, technical account manager or similar) and/or a growth engineer or product manager role with exposure to GTM systems
- Demonstrated experience building AI workflows, automation, or product solutions
- Strong product management instincts and systems thinking
- Genuine passion for GTM engineering and the future of AI-driven selling
- High bias to action and ownership mentality
- Technical skills (coding concepts, SQL and scripting languages, low-code tools)
- Understanding of B2B sales processes and metrics
- Must be located in New York or San Francisco

Nice to Haves: Salesforce/HubSpot experience, modern sales automation tools, Notion power user, sales operations background

Compensation: $150,000-$175,000 base. In-person collaboration required Mon/Tue/Thu (Anchor Days).

strengths.title: Built the GTM AI platform the role is responsible for creating
strengths.description: At Smarter Technologies I designed and deployed a 22-skill, 5-agent AI operating system specifically for a GTM team. The platform included skills and workflows for proposal and custom demo generation, discovery prep, competitive intelligence, product & market feedback, and more. It was not a prototype. It ran live on $31.8M in active pipeline and was used by nearly two dozen people on a daily basis. The role asks someone to build this kind of system at Notion; I have already done it from inside a commercial org.

strengths.title: Notion Meeting Intelligence: a Direct Demo
strengths.description: Built a multi-agent pipeline that automatically turns sales meeting recordings into structured deal intelligence and writes it into Notion including summaries, action items, market signals, and follow-up context. The system is precisely what a GTM AI Innovation Manager would pitch to Notion's own sales team as a productivity multiplier. I built it for mine.

strengths.title: Hooks-based usage tracking and adoption measurement
strengths.description: Built a hooks-based logging system on top of the agent platform. Every skill or tool invocation was captured, and 15-20% of usages prompted for structured feedback. Used that signal to identify which skills had friction and where. ROI measurement on AI and automation implementations is listed as a core 'What You'll Achieve' in the JD; I built the instrumentation for it.

strengths.title: Cross-functional bridge: technical to commercial
strengths.description: At both Thoughtful AI and Smarter Technologies I operated as the translator between engineering teams and field-facing commercial teams: gathering requirements from sellers, building systems, and driving adoption. The JD describes this as a core deliverable: 'bridge technical and commercial teams, gather technical requirements, ensure solutions deliver real business value.' That is my default operating mode.

weaknesses.title: Salesforce/HubSpot RevOps tooling depth
weaknesses.description: My AI automation work has been built on top of custom pipelines and Notion itself, not deeply inside Salesforce/HubSpot CRM workflows, which are listed as nice-to-haves.
weaknesses.mitigation: I did build a bi-directional sync between Salesforce and Notion for my GTM pipeline, but it's the only experience I have building on either platform. The platform and integration patterns are familiar, and in my experience the specific CRM tooling is rampable.

weaknesses.title: Internal tooling vs. revenue-facing role
weaknesses.description: My GTM AI work has been primarily building tools for my own team, not for a company's external sales motion at Notion's scale (millions of users, enterprise accounts like Toyota and OpenAI).
weaknesses.mitigation: I have built many custom demos for cusotmer-facing sales work and led a team that build out custom analytics views for each prospect in our pipeline based on their unique data. The design instincts transfer directly. I've found building bespoke works well in an age where code is increasingly cheap, in fact, bespoke is often much more personal for buyers.

referralBlurb: I'm referring Dan Mathieson for the GTM AI Innovation Manager role. Dan built a 22-skill, 5-agent AI operating system for his sales and GTM team at Smarter Technologies. The system is fully deployed, instrumented with usage tracking, and running on $31.8M in active pipeline. He also built the Notion Meeting Intelligence system, a multi-agent pipeline that turns meeting recordings into structured deal data written directly into Notion. This is the candidate who has already built what the role is responsible for designing, from inside a commercial GTM org.

projects.name: Notion Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: Built a multi-agent pipeline that turns meeting recordings into structured deal intelligence in Notion. Directly relevant to Notion's AI innovation motion.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: Production AI system for healthcare automation. Demonstrates the enterprise AI deployment experience behind the GTM positioning.
